title: Remove Tip Or A-Axis PCA Connections And Access Panel
summary: Disconnect the documented Tip or A-Axis PCA connections and remove the station side panel if needed as part of the source-backed PCA removal procedure. The source-backed actions in this excerpt cover lockout/tagout, side panel removal for access, and disconnecting the FFC, three green terminal blocks, and two Molex connectors.
when_to_use: Use when performing the documented removal portion of the Tip (or A-Axis) PCA replacement procedure and access is needed to the PCA and its electrical connections.
not_for: Do not use for steps beyond the documented removal actions in this source excerpt.
Do not use when the required lockout/tagout procedure cannot be confirmed.
Do not use when connector identification or original connection locations cannot be confirmed from the source and equipment state.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform lockout/tagout, remove the side panel if needed, identify and disconnect the documented PCA connections, and preserve connector location information for later reinstallation.

safety_notes: LOTO the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure on page 2 before beginning removal work.
This procedure includes component disconnection work and is not marked support-safe.
access_or_tools_needed: Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure on page 2
Visual and physical access to the station side panel and PCA

access_required: Visual and physical access to the station side panel and PCA
system_preconditions: Tipper is locked out/tagged out using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.
Side panel access is available if needed to reach the PCA.
external_references: Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure on page 2

visual_references.description: Tip PCA maintenance diagram near section 7.3.17.1 Removal; use for PCA identification and general connector/access orientation.
Flat-Flex Cable figure showing the FFC and related A-axis PCA connection context.
Tip cable figure with nearby removal context including side panel access.

steps.title: Lock out and tag out the tipper
steps.purpose: Place the equipment in the required safe state before panel removal and connector disconnection.
steps.instruction: LOTO the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure on page 2 before performing any removal or disconnection work.
steps.expected_result: The tipper is in the documented lockout/tagout state required for PCA removal work.
steps.healthy_condition: Lockout/tagout has been applied in accordance with the referenced procedure before any access or disconnection steps begin.
steps.failure_condition: The lockout/tagout state cannot be confirmed or has not been applied.

actions.label: Apply LOTO
actions.exact_action: LOTO the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure on page 2.

stop_or_escalate_if: The required Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure cannot be confirmed.
The tipper cannot be placed in the required lockout/tagout state.

source_evidence.text: LOTO the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure on page 2.
The removal procedure begins with LOTO of the tipper.

steps.title: Remove the station side panel if needed
steps.purpose: Gain access to the Tip or A-Axis PCA area.
steps.instruction: If necessary, use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the side panel, then remove the station side panel to access the PCA area.
steps.expected_result: The station side panel is removed when needed and the PCA area is accessible.
steps.healthy_condition: The side panel is removed without ambiguity and the PCA area can be reached for connector disconnection.
steps.failure_condition: The side panel cannot be removed, the fasteners cannot be identified, or access to the PCA area is still blocked.

actions.label: Remove side panel fasteners
actions.exact_action: Use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the side panel.

actions.label: Remove side panel
actions.exact_action: Remove the station side panel to access the PCA area.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Tip PCA area and general PCA location after side panel removal.
Nearby side panel access context showing removal of nine self-tapping screws to access internal tipper components.

stop_or_escalate_if: The side panel fasteners or access method cannot be confirmed from the source and equipment state.
Removing the side panel does not provide clear access to the PCA area.

source_evidence.text: If necessary, use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the side panel, then remove the station side panel to access the motor.
The removal procedure ... may require removing nine self-tapping fasteners from the side panel to access internal components.
The removal procedure instructs the technician to LOTO the tipper, remove nine self-tapping screws from the side panel if needed to access the motor.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-18 appears on page 173 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_17_1_removal.

steps.title: Unplug the FFC from the PCA
steps.purpose: Disconnect the flat-flex cable from the PCA as part of the documented removal sequence.
steps.instruction: Unplug the FFC from the PCA.
steps.expected_result: The FFC is disconnected from the PCA.
steps.healthy_condition: The FFC is fully unplugged from the PCA and the connection point is clearly identified.
steps.failure_condition: The FFC connection cannot be positively identified or the cable cannot be unplugged as documented.

actions.label: Disconnect FFC
actions.exact_action: Unplug the FFC from the PCA.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: FFC identification and A-axis PCA connection context.
General Tip PCA location to orient the FFC connection area.

stop_or_escalate_if: The FFC connection on the PCA cannot be identified with confidence from the source and equipment state.
The cable or connector does not match the documented FFC connection.

source_evidence.text: Unplug the FFC from the PCA.
At the A-axis PCA the technician ... unplugs the old flat-flex cable.

steps.title: Unplug the three green terminal blocks and record their locations
steps.purpose: Disconnect the terminal block connections while preserving their original placement for later reinstallation.
steps.instruction: Unplug the three green terminal blocks, and note where each one was plugged in.
steps.expected_result: All three green terminal blocks are disconnected and their original locations are recorded or otherwise preserved.
steps.healthy_condition: Each of the three green terminal blocks has been unplugged and its original PCA location is known.
steps.failure_condition: One or more terminal blocks cannot be identified, disconnected, or matched to its original location.

actions.label: Disconnect green terminal blocks
actions.exact_action: Unplug the three green terminal blocks.

actions.label: Note terminal block locations
actions.exact_action: Note where each one was plugged in.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Tip PCA layout for identifying the green terminal block connection area.

stop_or_escalate_if: The three green terminal blocks cannot be positively identified.
The original connection locations cannot be preserved or confirmed before disconnection.

source_evidence.text: Unplug the three green terminal blocks, and note where each one was plugged in.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-18 appears on page 173 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_17_1_removal.

steps.title: Unplug the two Molex connectors
steps.purpose: Complete the documented PCA electrical disconnection steps in this source excerpt.
steps.expected_result: Both Molex connectors are disconnected from the PCA assembly.
steps.healthy_condition: The two Molex connectors are unplugged and no additional undocumented disconnection is performed.
steps.failure_condition: The Molex connectors cannot be identified or disconnected as documented.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Tip PCA layout for general Molex connector location and orientation.

stop_or_escalate_if: The two Molex connectors cannot be positively identified from the source and equipment state.
Additional undocumented connectors appear to require removal.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-18 appears on page 173 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_17_1_removal.

success_criteria: The tipper has been locked out/tagged out using the referenced procedure.
The station side panel has been removed if needed for access.
The FFC has been unplugged from the PCA.
The three green terminal blocks have been unplugged and their original locations noted.
The two Molex connectors have been unplugged.
The PCA electrical connections covered by this excerpt are disconnected in preparation for further PCA replacement work.
healthy_conditions: The equipment is in the required LOTO state before work begins.
The PCA area is accessible after side panel removal if needed.
Each documented connector type can be positively identified before disconnection.
Original terminal block locations are preserved for later reinstallation.
failure_conditions: The side panel or its fasteners cannot be removed as documented.
The FFC, green terminal blocks, or Molex connectors cannot be positively identified.
Original connector locations cannot be confirmed or preserved.
The task would require proceeding beyond the documented removal actions in this source excerpt.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the required lockout/tagout procedure cannot be confirmed.
Escalate if connector identification cannot be confirmed from the source and equipment state.
Escalate if original connection locations cannot be determined before disconnecting the terminal blocks.
Do not proceed beyond the documented removal actions in this source excerpt.
